===========================================================
  ASSAM TEACHER ELIGIBILITY TEST (TET)
  COMPLETE SYLLABUS -- PAPER 1 & PAPER 2
===========================================================

>> PAPER 1 -- For Classes I to V (Primary Level)
   All questions compulsory -- 150 MCQs, 150 marks, 2.5 hours

----------------------------------------------------------
Module 1: Child Development & Pedagogy (30 questions x 1 mark = 30 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Child Development -- Piaget, Kohlberg, Vygotsky theories
* Learning -- Concept, Nature, Factors affecting learning
* Intelligence -- Thorndike, Spearman, Gardner, Sternberg
* Motivation -- Intrinsic & Extrinsic, Maslow's hierarchy
* Personality -- Types, Traits, Assessment
* Inclusive Education -- CWSN, Gifted children, Remedial teaching
* Teaching Methods -- Lecture, Discussion, Activity-based, Play-way
* Learning Difficulties & Educational Guidance
* Assessment & Evaluation -- Formative, Summative, CCE
* Right to Education Act 2009

----------------------------------------------------------
Module 2: Language I (Assamese / Bodo / Bengali / Hindi) (30 questions x 1 mark = 30 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Unseen Prose Passage -- Comprehension questions
* Grammar -- Sandhi, Samas, Karaka, Tense, Case, Gender, Number
* Vocabulary -- Synonyms, Antonyms, Idioms, Phrases
* Composition -- Letter writing, Essay, Paragraph
* Pedagogy -- Methods of language teaching, Skills (LSRW)

----------------------------------------------------------
Module 3: Language II (English) (30 questions x 1 mark = 30 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Unseen Passage -- Reading Comprehension
* Grammar -- Tenses, Articles, Prepositions, Voices, Narration
* Vocabulary -- Synonyms, Antonyms, Word formation
* Pedagogical Issues -- Language acquisition, Teaching methods
* Teaching-Learning Materials -- Flashcards, Charts, Audio-visual aids
* Evaluation in English Language

----------------------------------------------------------
Module 4: Mathematics (30 questions x 1 mark = 30 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Number System -- Place value, Addition, Subtraction, Multiplication, Division
* Fractions & Decimals
* Geometry -- Shapes, Spatial understanding, Patterns
* Measurement -- Length, Weight, Capacity, Time, Money
* Data Handling -- Tables, Pictographs, Bar graphs
* Pedagogical Issues -- Teaching Maths at primary level, Error analysis
* Problem-solving & Reasoning

----------------------------------------------------------
Module 5: Environmental Studies (EVS) (30 questions x 1 mark = 30 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Family & Friends -- Relationships, Plants, Animals
* Food -- Sources, Nutrition, Balanced diet
* Shelter -- Types of houses, Clothing
* Water -- Sources, Uses, Conservation
* Travel -- Means of transport, Communication
* Our Surroundings -- Air, Sound, Light, Pollution
* Pedagogical Issues -- Teaching EVS, Integration with other subjects
* Environmental Awareness & Conservation

>> PAPER 2 -- For Classes VI to VIII (Upper Primary Level)
   All questions compulsory -- 150 MCQs, 150 marks, 2.5 hours

----------------------------------------------------------
Module 1: Child Development & Pedagogy (30 questions x 1 mark = 30 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
(Same as Paper 1 but with adolescent development focus)
* Adolescence -- Physical, Cognitive, Social, Emotional development
* Classroom management at upper primary level
* Curriculum & syllabus design
* Action research for teachers

----------------------------------------------------------
Module 2: Language I (Assamese / Bodo / Bengali / Hindi) (30 questions x 1 mark = 30 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
(Same pattern as Paper 1 -- higher difficulty level)
* Advanced grammar & composition
* Literary analysis -- Poetry, Prose, Drama excerpts

----------------------------------------------------------
Module 3: Language II (English) (30 questions x 1 mark = 30 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
(Same pattern as Paper 1 -- higher difficulty level)
* Advanced comprehension & grammar
* Teaching of English at upper primary level

----------------------------------------------------------
Module 4A: Mathematics & Science (for Mathematics & Science teachers) (60 questions x 1 mark = 60 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
Mathematics (30 questions):
* Number System -- Integers, Rational numbers, Squares, Square roots
* Algebra -- Expressions, Equations, Factorization, Linear equations
* Geometry -- Congruence, Similarity, Constructions, Mensuration
* Statistics -- Mean, Median, Mode, Probability
* Pedagogical Issues -- Teaching Mathematics at upper primary level

Science (30 questions):
* Physics -- Motion, Force, Friction, Sound, Light, Electricity, Magnetism
* Chemistry -- Matter, Elements, Compounds, Acids, Bases, Metals, Non-metals
* Biology -- Cell, Tissues, Plant & Animal Kingdom, Human Body, Health & Diseases
* Natural Resources & Environmental Science
* Pedagogical Issues -- Teaching Science, Laboratory work, Experiments

----------------------------------------------------------
Module 4B: Social Studies (for Social Studies teachers) (60 questions x 1 mark = 60 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
History (20 questions):
* Ancient India -- Indus Valley, Vedic Age, Mauryas, Guptas
* Medieval India -- Delhi Sultanate, Mughals, Bhakti Movement
* Modern India -- British Rule, Freedom Struggle, Independence
* Assam History -- Ahoms, British annexation, Post-independence

Geography (20 questions):
* Physical Geography -- Earth, Atmosphere, Climate, Vegetation
* Geography of India -- Resources, Agriculture, Industries
* Geography of Assam -- Physical features, River systems, Demographics
* Environmental Geography -- Ecosystems, Conservation

Social & Political Science (20 questions):
* Indian Constitution -- Preamble, Fundamental Rights, Duties
* Governance -- Democracy, Panchayati Raj, Local Self-government
* Social Issues -- Caste, Gender, Poverty, Unemployment
* Pedagogical Issues -- Teaching Social Studies, Map work, Projects

[QUALIFYING MARKS] UR 60% (90 marks), OBC/SC/ST 50% (75 marks)
[NOTE] No negative marking
[NOTE] Valid for 7 years for all categories

===========================================================
  APSC COMBINED COMPETITIVE EXAMINATION (CCE)
  COMPLETE SYLLABUS
===========================================================

>> PRELIMINARY EXAMINATION (Objective Type -- 200 + 200 marks)

----------------------------------------------------------
Paper 1: General Studies (200 marks, 2 hours)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Current Events of National & International Importance
* History of India & Indian National Movement
* Indian & World Geography -- Physical, Social, Economic
* Indian Polity & Governance -- Constitution, Political System, Panchayati Raj, Public Policy, Rights Issues
* Economic & Social Development -- Sustainable Development, Poverty, Inclusion, Demographics, Social Sector Initiatives
* General Issues on Environmental Ecology, Bio-diversity & Climate Change
* General Science

----------------------------------------------------------
Paper 2: CSAT -- Civil Services Aptitude Test (200 marks, 2 hours)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Comprehension
* Interpersonal & Communication Skills
* Logical Reasoning & Analytical Ability
* Decision Making & Problem Solving
* General Mental Ability
* Basic Numeracy (Class X level -- Arithmetic, Algebra, Geometry, Statistics)
* English Language Comprehension (Class X level)

[IMPORTANT] CSAT is QUALIFYING in nature -- minimum 33% required.

>> MAIN EXAMINATION (Conventional/Essay Type)

----------------------------------------------------------
Paper 1: General English (300 marks, 3 hours)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Essay writing
* Precis writing
* Grammar & usage
* Comprehension of given passages
* Translation from Assamese/Bengali/Bodo to English & vice versa

----------------------------------------------------------
Paper 2: General Studies 1 (300 marks, 3 hours)
----------------------------------------------------------
* History of India & Assam with special emphasis on the freedom movement
* Geography of India, Assam & the North-East region
* Society, Culture & Heritage of Assam
* Social & Religious reform movements in India & Assam

----------------------------------------------------------
Paper 3: General Studies 2 (300 marks, 3 hours)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Indian Constitution & Polity
* Governance, Administration & Public Policy
* Social Justice -- Welfare schemes, Health, Education, Human Resources
* International Relations -- India's foreign policy, Neighbourhood, Global groupings
* Role of NITI Aayog, Finance Commission, CAG, Election Commission

----------------------------------------------------------
Paper 4: General Studies 3 (300 marks, 3 hours)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Economy of India & Assam -- Planning, Growth, Budget, Banking, Sectors
* Agriculture -- Cropping patterns, Irrigation, Storage, Marketing, SDP
* Science & Technology -- Developments, IT, Space, Bio-tech
* Environment -- Conservation, Climate Change, Disaster Management
* Security -- Internal security, Cyber security, Border management

----------------------------------------------------------
Paper 5: General Studies 4 (300 marks, 3 hours)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Ethics & Human Interface -- Essence, Determinants, Consequences of Ethics
* Attitude -- Content, Structure, Moral & Political Attitudes
* Aptitude & Foundational Values for Civil Service -- Integrity, Impartiality, Non-partisanship, Objectivity
* Emotional Intelligence -- Concepts, Utility & Application
* Case Studies on Ethical Dilemmas

----------------------------------------------------------
Paper 6 & 7: Optional Subject (2 papers x 300 marks each = 600 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
Choose ONE from: Assamese, Bodo, Bengali, Economics, Geography, History, Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Botany, Zoology, Political Science, Sociology, Philosophy, Education, Commerce, Anthropology, Statistics, Home Science, Management, Public Administration, Law, Arabic, Persian, Sanskrit, Hindi, English, Burmese, Tibetan, Nepali, Khasi, Garo, Mizo

>> INTERVIEW / PERSONALITY TEST (275 marks)

----------------------------------------------------------
* Assessment of candidate's personality, intellectual qualities, social traits, leadership
* Interest in current affairs, mental alertness, critical thinking

[TOTAL] Prelims (Qualifying) + Mains 1800 + Interview 275 = 2075 marks

titleEn: APSC CCE 2026 Preparation - Top 3 Must-Buy Books from Amazon
subject: General Studies
description:
Why These Books?

Preparing for the APSC Combined Competitive Examination (CCE) requires the right set of books that cover the syllabus comprehensively. Based on the APSC CCE syllabus analysis, top rankers' recommendations, and coverage of both Prelims and Mains papers, here are the 3 most essential books every APSC aspirant must buy from Amazon to start their preparation in 2026.

These books cover Indian Polity, Assam History, and Assam General Knowledge - the three pillars of the APSC CCE syllabus where 40-50% of questions are asked.

Book 1: Indian Polity by M. Laxmikanth (7th Edition)

Indian Polity for UPSC & State PCS

Author: M. Laxmikanth

Publisher: McGraw Hill

Edition: 7th (July 2023) | Pages: 840

MRP: Rs 1,045

Amazon Price: Rs 509 (approx)

Rating: 4.6 / 5.0 (Amazon)

Buy on Amazon

Overview

Indian Polity by M. Laxmikanth is the undisputed bible for Indian Polity preparation. The 7th edition contains 92 chapters covering the entire political and constitutional spectrum of India, with 12 new chapters added including Law Commission, Bar Council, Delimitation Commission, World Constitutions, Landmark Judgements, and more. It is the single most recommended book by UPSC and APSC toppers across India.

Strengths

  • [+] Comprehensive coverage: 92 chapters covering every aspect of Indian Constitution, governance, and political system - no need for any supplementary polity book
  • [+] Exam-oriented content: Includes previous years' questions of both Preliminary and Main exams with detailed explanations
  • [+] Lucid language: Written in simple, easy-to-understand English that makes complex constitutional concepts accessible
  • [+] Updated content: 7th edition includes recent constitutional amendments, Supreme Court judgments, and government policies up to 2023
  • [+] Bonus online access: Comes with McGraw Hill Edge platform access - practice tests, conceptual videos, and expert sessions
  • [+] 8 appendices: Additional reference material on key constitutional documents and acts

Weaknesses

  • [-] Lengthy for beginners: At 840 pages, it can feel overwhelming for first-time readers - requires disciplined reading plan
  • [-] Limited depth on Assam-specific polity: Covers Indian Polity broadly but does not address Assam-specific administrative structures in detail
  • [-] Heavy weight: Weighs over 1 kg - not very portable for daily commuting
  • [-] 7th edition is the latest but published July 2023: Some very recent developments (2024-2026) may need supplementing with current affairs
Verdict: A must-buy for every APSC aspirant. This single book covers the entire Polity portion of APSC CCE Prelims Paper 1 and Mains GS Paper 2. Rating: 9.5/10

Book 2: A History of Assam by Sir Edward Gait (Revised Second Edition)

A History of Assam: Revised Second Edition

Author: Sir Edward Gait

Publisher: Dev Publishers & Distributors

Edition: Revised Second (2025) | Pages: 305

Amazon Price: Rs 495

Rating: 4.4 / 5.0 (Amazon)

Buy on Amazon

Overview

First published in 1905, A History of Assam by Sir Edward Gait remains the most authoritative and foundational text on Assam's history. This revised second edition (2025) offers a comprehensive survey of Assam's history from ancient times through the Ahom rule, Mughal invasions, Burmese wars, and the colonial period. It is the single most important reference for the Assam-specific history section of the APSC CCE syllabus.

Strengths

  • [+] Unmatched authority: Written by Sir Edward Gait, a British civil servant who served in Assam and had direct access to original Buranjis (Ahom chronicles), inscriptions, and administrative records
  • [+] Comprehensive coverage: Covers the Ahom kingdom in detail - their administration, the Paik system, Saraighat battle, Mughal conflicts - all directly relevant to APSC CCE
  • [+] Ethnographic detail: Extensive coverage of Assam's tribes - Khasis, Nagas, Garos, Chutias, Cacharis - important for APSC anthropology and sociology portions
  • [+] Revised edition: The 2025 edition has been newly composed with clearer typography and organization while preserving the original content
  • [+] Primary source-based: Draws from ancient Sanskrit sources, copper-plate inscriptions, and Ahom Buranjis - provides authentic, exam-relevant information

Weaknesses

  • [-] Colonial perspective: Written from a British colonial viewpoint - some interpretations may feel dated and need to be balanced with modern historiography
  • [-] Dense academic language: The early 20th century prose can be challenging for some readers compared to modern textbooks
  • [-] Limited post-independence coverage: The book focuses primarily on pre-1947 Assam; post-independence political developments need other sources
  • [-] No practice questions: Unlike modern exam books, this is a pure historical text with no MCQs or chapter summaries for quick revision
Verdict: Indispensable for APSC CCE. 30-35% of the APSC Prelims and Mains GS papers focus on Assam-specific topics, and this book is the gold standard for Assam history. Rating: 9/10

UPSC Civil Services 2026

Top 3 Must-Buy Books from Amazon

Prelims & Mains | English Medium | 2025-2026 Editions

Why These 3 Books?

Cracking the UPSC Civil Services Examination requires mastering a vast syllabus across Polity, History, Geography, Economy, Science & Technology, and Current Affairs. While there are dozens of books for each subject, these three titles form the non-negotiable foundation for every serious UPSC aspirant. Indian Polity by M. Laxmikanth is the undisputed bible for polity questions - covering the Constitution, Parliament, Judiciary, and governance with exceptional clarity. India's Ancient Past by R.S. Sharma provides the authoritative NCERT-level bedrock for ancient and medieval history, written by one of India's most respected historians. Geography of India by Majid Husain (11th Edition, co-authored by Pallavi Saxena) is the most comprehensive single-volume resource for Indian geography, updated for the 2026 exam pattern. Together, these three books cover approximately 35-40% of the UPSC Prelims syllabus and build the conceptual framework needed for Mains answer writing.

by M. Laxmikanth

Overview

Indian Polity by M. Laxmikanth is the definitive guide for UPSC Polity and Governance. This 8th Courseware edition has been thoroughly updated to reflect the latest constitutional amendments, Supreme Court judgments, and parliamentary developments up to 2025. It covers the entire UPSC Polity syllabus - from the historical background of the Constitution to contemporary governance issues - in a structured, topic-wise format. Each chapter includes solved previous years' questions (PYQs) from Prelims (2013-2025) and Mains (2013-2025), practice questions, and 40+ conceptual video lectures via McGraw Hill EDGE. The book also features a colour e-book for digital reference, making it an indispensable resource for both Prelims and Mains preparation.

  • 8th Courseware edition includes solved PYQs from 2013-2025 (Prelims & Mains)

  • Some topics (e.g., international relations, social justice) are covered briefly and require additional reference books

Overview

India's Ancient Past by Professor R.S. Sharma - one of India's most eminent historians - is the gold standard for ancient Indian history preparation. This Oxford University Press edition takes readers on a comprehensive journey from the Neolithic and Chalcolithic ages through the Harappan Civilization, Vedic period, rise of Buddhism and Jainism, Mauryan Empire, Gupta period, Satavahanas, and up to the times of Harshavardhana. Written in a lucid, narrative style, the book seamlessly weaves together political history with social, economic, and cultural developments. It addresses critical historiographical debates (such as the Aryan migration theory and the identity of the Harappan culture) that frequently appear in UPSC Mains questions. The final chapter on the transition from Ancient to Medieval India provides a crucial bridge for understanding the continuity of Indian civilization. While originally designed as an undergraduate textbook, its analytical depth and accessible prose make it ideal for UPSC aspirants seeking a strong conceptual foundation.

Strengths

  • Authored by R.S. Sharma - the most respected authority on Ancient Indian history and former Chairman of ICHR
  • Covers the complete ancient India syllabus for UPSC in a single, concise volume (295 pages)
  • Analytical approach that explores cause-effect relationships, not just factual listing - ideal for Mains answer writing
  • Affordable price (Rs.370) - excellent value for a foundational UPSC reference book

Weaknesses

  • Does not cover Medieval India (Satish Chandra) or Modern India (Bipin Chandra) - you need all three periods separately
  • Some readers find the Marxist historiographical perspective requires supplementation with alternate viewpoints for balanced answers
  • Limited practice questions / MCQs - not designed as an exam-prep book; requires separate question bank for practice
Verdict: 9.0 / 10 - The best single-volume introduction to Ancient Indian history for UPSC. Read alongside NCERT Class 11 Ancient India for maximum retention.

This 11th edition has been completely restructured to align with the latest UPSC CSE 2026 syllabus and examination pattern. It covers 17 comprehensive chapters spanning geological structure, physiography, drainage systems, climate, soils, agriculture, mineral and energy resources, industries, transport, population, settlements, regional planning, and contemporary issues. The edition features solved PYQs (Prelims & Mains), trend analysis, colour maps and diagrams, chapter-end exercises, and model answers. A standout feature is the inclusion of contemporary and niche topics like Somalian jet streams, coral bleaching, Blue Economy, Cyber Warfare, Gift City, micro-irrigation, and climate change - topics frequently tested in recent UPSC papers. The book also provides free access to McGraw Hill EDGE platform with 30+ video lectures, monthly current affairs updates, webinars, and curated study material.

Strengths

Weaknesses

  • Some users find certain topics over-detailed for Prelims-level preparation; needs judicious skipping
  • Does not cover World Geography / Physical Geography basics (Goh Cheng Leong recommended as a companion)
Verdict: 9.2 / 10 - The definitive Indian Geography book for UPSC 2026. Use with Goh Cheng Leong for Physical Geography and Atlas for map-based questions.

Study Strategy: How to Use These Books

  1. Start with NCERTs (Foundation): Before diving into these advanced books, read the corresponding NCERT textbooks (Class 6-12) for each subject. NCERTs build the conceptual framework that makes Laxmikanth, R.S. Sharma, and Majid Husain much easier to absorb. Spend 2-3 weeks on NCERTs for Polity, History, and Geography.
  2. Subject-wise Cyclic Approach (Months 1-3): Study one subject at a time. Begin with Indian Polity (Laxmikanth) - read 2 chapters daily, solve chapter-end MCQs. Then move to Ancient History (R.S. Sharma) - read 1 chapter daily, make timeline notes. Finally tackle Geography (Majid Husain) - read 1 chapter daily, focus on maps and diagrams. Complete this cycle in 3 months.
  3. Integrate with Current Affairs (Months 2-6): From Month 2 onwards, subscribe to a monthly current affairs magazine (e.g., Yojana, Kurukshetra, or a coaching institute magazine). Link current affairs topics back to the relevant chapters in your three books. For example, a news item about the Ganga cleanup connects to Drainage Systems in Geography of India, and a parliamentary bill connects to Law-Making Process in Laxmikanth.
  4. Practice PYQs and Mock Tests (Months 4-8): After completing each book, solve 10 years of UPSC Prelims PYQs for that subject. Use the solved PYQs in Laxmikanth and Majid Husain to identify repeated themes. For R.S. Sharma, use a separate history PYQ compilation. Take full-length mock tests every 2 weeks to build stamina and identify weak areas.
  5. Revision and Consolidation (Months 7-9): Create short revision notes (2-3 pages per subject) from all three books. Focus on: (a) Polity - key articles, schedules, landmark judgments, (b) History - important dynasties, timelines, cultural developments, (c) Geography - maps, climate patterns, agriculture, mineral belts. Revise these notes every weekend. In the final 2 months before Prelims, only revise notes and solved PYQs.

  COMPLETE SYLLABUS -- TIER 1 & TIER 2

>> TIER 1 -- Preliminary Examination (Objective -- 200 marks, 60 minutes)

----------------------------------------------------------
Section 1: General Intelligence & Reasoning (50 questions x 2 marks = 50 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Analogies
* Similarities & Differences
* Space Visualization
* Problem Solving
* Analysis & Judgment
* Decision Making
* Visual Memory
* Discrimination
* Observation
* Relationship Concepts
* Arithmetical Reasoning
* Verbal & Figure Classification
* Arithmetical Number Series
* Non-verbal Reasoning (Embedded Figures, Mirror Images, Paper Folding)

----------------------------------------------------------
Section 2: General Awareness (50 questions x 2 marks = 50 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Current Affairs -- National & International
* History of India & Freedom Struggle
* Geography -- Physical, Economic, Social
* Indian Polity & Constitution
* Economic Scene
* General Science (up to 10th standard)
* Environmental & Ecological Issues
* Sports, Awards, Books & Authors, Important Personalities

----------------------------------------------------------
Section 3: Quantitative Aptitude (50 questions x 2 marks = 50 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Number System -- LCM, HCF, Divisibility, Surds & Indices
* Arithmetic -- Percentage, Profit & Loss, Discount, Simple & Compound Interest
* Ratio & Proportion, Partnership
* Time, Speed & Distance
* Time & Work, Pipes & Cisterns
* Algebra -- Polynomials, Linear & Quadratic Equations
* Geometry -- Lines, Angles, Triangles, Circles, Quadrilaterals
* Mensuration -- Areas, Volumes, Surface areas
* Trigonometry -- Ratios, Heights & Distances
* Statistics -- Mean, Median, Mode, Standard Deviation
* Data Interpretation -- Tables, Bar graphs, Line graphs, Pie charts

----------------------------------------------------------
Section 4: English Comprehension (50 questions x 2 marks = 50 marks)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Spot the Error
* Fill in the Blanks
* Synonyms & Antonyms
* Idioms & Phrases
* One-word Substitution
* Improvement of Sentences
* Active/Passive Voice
* Direct/Indirect Speech
* Para jumbles
* Cloze Test
* Reading Comprehension

>> TIER 2 -- Mains Examination (Descriptive & Objective)

----------------------------------------------------------
Paper 1: Quantitative Abilities (200 marks, 2 hours)
----------------------------------------------------------
* All topics from Tier 1 Quantitative Aptitude -- in greater depth
* Advanced Algebra, Geometry, Trigonometry, Mensuration
* Data Interpretation -- more complex charts & tables
* Coordinate Geometry, Probability

----------------------------------------------------------
Paper 2: English Language & Comprehension (200 marks, 2 hours)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Advanced Reading Comprehension
* Essay Writing (250-300 words)
* Precis Writing
* Grammar -- Advanced error detection, sentence improvement
* Vocabulary -- Context-based word meanings, usage

----------------------------------------------------------
Paper 3: Statistics (200 marks, 2 hours) -- Only for SO (Statistical Investigator)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Collection & Presentation of Data
* Measures of Central Tendency
* Measures of Dispersion
* Correlation & Regression
* Probability Theory
* Sampling Theory
* Index Numbers
* Time Series Analysis

----------------------------------------------------------
Paper 4: General Studies (Finance & Economics) (200 marks, 2 hours) -- Only for SO (Audit/Accounts)
----------------------------------------------------------
* Indian Economy -- Planning, GDP, Budget, Taxation, Fiscal Policy
* Financial System -- RBI, Banks, Capital Market, Insurance
* Accounting Principles -- Journal, Ledger, Trial Balance, Final Accounts
* Auditing -- Principles, Internal Check, Vouching, Verification
* Cost Accounting -- Elements of Cost, Marginal Costing, Budgetary Control

[NOTE] Negative marking: 0.50 marks deducted for each wrong answer (Tier 1), varies for Tier 2
[NOTE] Tier 1 is SCREENING in nature; marks are considered for final merit.

Why These 3 Books for TET Assam?

Preparing for the TET Assam exam (Upper Primary & Lower Primary) requires a strategic approach and the right study materials. After analyzing the syllabus, exam pattern, and candidate feedback, we have curated a list of the top 3 must-buy books for TET Assam 2026. These books cover Child Development & Pedagogy, Assam-specific General Knowledge, and a comprehensive TET guide covering all subjects. Each book has been selected based on content quality, alignment with the official syllabus, affordability, and positive reviews from past successful candidates.

Book 1: Assam TET (Teacher Eligibility Test) for Secondary Education Exam Guide

Amazon Price: ?328

ASIN: 9350123339

Publisher: Ramesh Publishing House | Edition: 2020th | Pages: 396 | Rating: 3.4/5

Buy on Amazon

Overview

This book by RPH Editorial Board (Popular Master Guide series) is specifically designed for candidates appearing in the Assam TET (Teacher Eligibility Test) for Secondary Education. It covers both Part I and Part II of the exam syllabus in a structured manner. The book includes specialised study material, solved question papers, chapterwise topic discussions in a lucid manner, and sufficient numbers of solved MCQs with detailed explanatory answers. It is an excellent resource for understanding the exam pattern and practicing extensively.

Strengths

  • Comprehensive coverage of both Part I and Part II syllabus
  • Includes solved previous year question paper for pattern familiarity
  • Chapterwise MCQs with detailed explanatory answers
  • Affordable price point at just ?328
  • Published by trusted Ramesh Publishing House with decades of exam guide expertise

Weaknesses

  • Some topics could benefit from more in-depth coverage
  • Limited focus on Assam-specific pedagogy compared to state-specific books
  • Edition is from 2020, awaiting a newer update

Verdict

An excellent all-in-one guide for TET Assam Secondary Education aspirants. Pair it with subject-specific books for a complete preparation strategy. Highly recommended for its structured approach and practice MCQs.

Book 2: Child Development & Pedagogy for CTET & STET (Paper 1 & 2) with Past Questions

Amazon Price: ?210

ASIN: 9390711959

Publisher: Disha Publication | Edition: 4th | Pages: 318 | Rating: 4.2/5

Overview

Child Development & Pedagogy is a core subject for both Paper 1 and Paper 2 of TET exams. This book by Disha Experts provides an exclusive treatment to the subject with special emphasis on Child Development, Inclusive Education, Learning, and Pedagogical Issues. The 4th edition has been updated with past CTET & STET papers from 2016 onwards. The book is divided into 10 well-structured chapters, each followed by previous year questions and a Test Yourself section with carefully selected MCQs. This book is aligned with CBSE/NCERT/NCF 2005 guidelines.

Strengths

  • Comprehensive coverage of all CDP topics with exhaustive theory
  • Includes actual previous year CTET & STET questions chapter-wise
  • Test Yourself MCQs for thorough practice after each chapter
  • High rating of 4.2/5 from 213+ ratings
  • Useful for both CTET and all State TET exams including Assam TET

Weaknesses

  • Not Assam TET specific - covers generic CDP syllabus
  • Some candidates may find the theory too concise for certain topics

Verdict

The best CDP book available for TET preparation. Since CDP syllabus is common across CTET and all State TETs, this book is a perfect choice. Must-buy for every TET Assam aspirant.

Study Strategy for TET Assam 2026

Phase 1: Build Foundation (Months 1-2)

Start with Book 1 (RPH TET Guide) to understand the complete exam pattern and syllabus. Simultaneously read Book 3 (Assam GK) chapter by chapter as it requires consistent memorization. Allocate 1 hour daily for Assam GK revision.

Phase 2: Master CDP (Month 3)

Dedicate this month entirely to Book 2 (Disha CDP). CDP carries significant weightage in both Paper 1 and Paper 2. Solve all previous year questions and practice tests. Revise concepts using the infographics and summaries.

Phase 3: Practice & Revision (Months 4-5)

Solve mock tests from all three books. Focus on weak areas. Use the Assam GK mock tests for self-assessment. Revise CDP concepts through the Test Yourself exercises. Practice time management by solving full-length papers.

Pro Tips

  • Create short notes for Assam GK - focus on districts, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks, and schemes
  • For CDP, understand Piaget, Vygotsky, Kohlberg theories thoroughly - they are frequently asked
  • Solve at least 10 previous year papers before the exam
  • Join online discussion forums for TET Assam to stay updated
